TJ Ware for Congress

A cleaner, more credible campaign platform for winning trust in North Texas.

The rebuild keeps the campaign's story and action paths intact, then presents them with stronger hierarchy, clearer credibility, and a much more premium conversion flow.

Official campaign website for TJ Ware for Congress | Texas District 24

JoinDonate
TJ Ware for Congress | Texas District 24

North Texas deserves a representative who is present, prepared, and hard to buy.

TJ Ware is running for Congress with a story rooted in service, work, advocacy, and family. The site is built to make that credibility legible and turn support into action across TX-24.

Donate nowJoin the teamInvite TJ
Take the voter surveyReview media and proofExplore district priorities

Marine Corps Iraq veteran

Pilot, electrician, roofer, and small-business operator

Consumer advocate and father of ten

Campaign posture

High trust. Local focus.

Why it matters

The site is organized around credibility first, then conversion. That is what makes a campaign feel serious instead of donation hungry.

10

children and a direct stake in the future

The family story gives the campaign real stakes and keeps the message grounded in what policy means at home.

CBS + NBCDFW

public proof already in market

Coverage tied to crisis response and community action raises credibility much faster than campaign copy alone.

2

live contribution routes supporters already trust

ActBlue and Anedot are both already available, which lets the rebuild focus on confidence and clarity instead of new payment plumbing.

TX-24

one district, one focused story

Everything is organized around North Texas families, employers, neighborhoods, and a campaign built to show up locally.

Why this campaign lands

A cleaner case for credibility, local relevance, and disciplined action.

The strongest campaign sites build confidence before they push a hard ask. This section makes the case for why this campaign deserves attention in the first place.

Credibility over theater

A campaign that feels earned instead of manufactured.

The strongest case for TJ Ware comes from service, work, advocacy, and visible action, not generic political staging.

District first

Grounded in North Texas families, employers, and neighborhoods.

The site keeps returning to affordability, healthcare access, growth, resilience, and everyday public trust in TX-24.

Action oriented

Built to turn attention into donations, volunteers, events, and local visibility.

The conversion architecture is designed to be clean and disciplined, not noisy or consultant-made.

Quick proof

Reasons supporters do not have to guess who TJ is.

Worked with the U.S. Department of Justice before age 17
Enlisted after 9/11 and served in Iraq with the Marine Corps
Used the GI Bill to become an instrument-rated pilot
Built and operated businesses across multiple states
Read TJ's story
Campaign narrative

A story architecture built for supporters, donors, and undecided voters.

The best biography sections are not autobiography dumps. They are clean story beats that explain why a candidate feels serious and why that matters now.

Built around work

The story starts with labor, family, and real-world responsibility.

TJ Ware's public narrative begins in Fort Worth around trades work, family pressure, small-business hustle, and early exposure to public institutions.

Shaped by service

Military service after 9/11 still anchors the campaign's seriousness.

The Marine Corps chapter gives the story a weight that feels lived, disciplined, and harder to dismiss as politics by branding.

Validated in the field

Aviation, business, disaster response, and advocacy keep the message grounded.

That mix of operator experience helps the campaign read more like a high-agency public servant and less like a media product.

Built for TX-24

The campaign should feel like it belongs to real North Texas people, not a template.

This campaign is strongest when the site makes it obvious who it is built for and what it is trying to fix in District 24.

Working families

Built for parents, caregivers, and households feeling pressure every month.

Affordability, healthcare access, education, and housing fairness are not side notes. They are part of the campaign's central argument.

Veterans and service-minded Texans

Seriousness comes through when service is part of the candidate's real biography.

The campaign has an easier time earning trust from voters who care about discipline, sacrifice, and public duty.

Builders and employers

Small-business operators, tradespeople, and problem solvers need representation too.

TJ's operator background gives the economic message a practical tone that feels closer to the district's working reality.

Voters tired of corruption

This campaign is strongest when it sounds allergic to insider games and public gaslighting.

The trust-and-accountability lane is not just rhetorical. It is one of the cleanest strategic contrasts in the entire platform.

Family economics

Lower pressure on housing, healthcare, and day-to-day costs.

The public platform keeps coming back to middle-class breathing room: wages, affordability, housing access, and a less rigged economy.

Growth with backbone

Support North Texas business growth without selling out the public.

District 24 is framed as a place of builders, employers, and working families that deserves practical growth instead of insider favoritism.

Prepared communities

Take disaster readiness and infrastructure resilience seriously.

Flood response, storm recovery, food security, and public preparedness appear repeatedly across the campaign's media and policy posture.

Fairness and safety

Push for public safety without abandoning fairness or civil rights.

The public message pairs accountability, anti-corruption, and sensible criminal-justice reform with a clear rejection of cruelty and performative chaos.

Explore district focusReview policy priorities
Media and proof

Coverage, interviews, and public record presented with more authority.

Media and public proof should do more than fill space. They should build confidence fast and make the campaign feel visible, active, and worth taking seriously.

CBS + NBCDFW

local television proof already in market

The most credible clips tie the campaign to visible response during real public emergencies and local pressure points.

Audacy

radio and long-form public explanation

Those interviews help the campaign feel more durable than a stream of shallow social posts.

YouTube

industry and issue-specific conversation

The archive supports a narrative of experience across insurance, roofing, recovery, and advocacy.

Live archive

one place for proof and press follow-up

The rebuilt media page acts like a front door, while the live archive remains available as source material.

CBS News Texas

Flood coverage tied to visible, on-the-ground response

The strongest local television proof positions TJ as active and present when communities are under pressure, not just politically visible when cameras are on.

NBCDFW

Winter storm response and public-service credibility

That coverage supports one of the clearest campaign contrasts: practical response, community action, and preparedness instead of performance politics.

Audacy and radio

Long-form conversation instead of stump-speech repetition

Radio and interview appearances make the campaign sound more substantive, more comfortable in detail, and more prepared to handle real questions.

YouTube and podcasts

A broader record across insurance, roofing, advocacy, and civic issues

Those conversations reinforce the operator profile and make the campaign feel more durable than a social-only presence.

Open the media pageView current live archive
Take action

Six clear ways to help the campaign build momentum.

The strongest homepage action section does not throw everything at the visitor at once. It gives each support lane a clear purpose and cleaner hierarchy.

Donate

Fund district outreach, creative production, and campaign momentum.

The fastest path for supporters who are ready to move the campaign from attention into execution.

Donate now

Volunteer

Join field outreach, events, visibility, or digital response.

The volunteer application is already live. The upgraded site simply makes the entry point feel far more credible and organized.

Join the team

Invite TJ

Bring TJ into a room, neighborhood, or coalition conversation.

Speaking invitations, community gatherings, and local organizing requests should be easy to start from the site.

Invite TJ

Voter survey

Tell the campaign what matters most across District 24.

The survey remains one of the best low-friction ways for local voters to shape the campaign conversation.

Take the survey

Signs and merch

Turn support into visible district presence.

Request yard signs, review merch, and help the campaign show up physically in neighborhoods and local events.

Request signs

Media and proof

Review the public record before you commit.

The media hub turns scattered proof points into a cleaner credibility surface for supporters, donors, and press.

Open media
Donate with confidence

Fund a serious district campaign without the cheap tactics.

The campaign already has trusted contribution routes in place. This rebuild simply makes the ask cleaner, more credible, and easier to act on.

Primary route

Use the campaign's clearest checkout path.

ActBlue remains the most familiar option for supporters who want a fast, campaign-native donation flow.

Give with ActBlue

Backup route

Use the second trusted processor if you prefer.

Anedot keeps a second path available for supporters who want a different payment experience or device behavior.

Give with Anedot

Visibility support

Pair your contribution with district presence.

Supporters who want to do more than donate can move directly into signs, merch, and visible campaign support.

Request signs

$10

Help cover small-dollar digital outreach.

A modest contribution can still support voter contact, updates, and campaign communication across the district.

Contribute

$25

Support literature, signs, and local visibility.

This level helps the campaign show up in neighborhoods, at events, and in direct voter contact materials.

Contribute

$50

Back event logistics and volunteer support.

Contributions at this level help power the real-world work required to host stops and activate supporters.

Contribute

$100

Fuel district travel, media, and campaign operations.

Larger gifts help the campaign move faster, communicate better, and sustain a more visible presence across TX-24.

Contribute
Final step

Help build a campaign platform that looks ready because it is ready.

Donate, join the team, invite TJ, or tell the campaign what matters most in your part of District 24.
